label: Clean-agent control: Jack drone privacy
source: single clean-context sub-agent, no kernel or assay context
actualProblem: The actual problem is not how to stop a drone. It is how to prevent a valuable image from existing before anyone has to touch the drone.

discoveryInsights.title: The attack is geometric
discoveryInsights.detail: The drone only needs line of sight to an exposed deck, tender transfer, beach club, hot tub, heli deck, or glass-heavy cabin.

discoveryInsights.title: The perimeter is a camera cone
discoveryInsights.detail: The real perimeter is not the hull; it is the camera cone from plausible launch points, chase boats, marinas, shorelines, cliffs, hotels, and public anchorages.

discoveryInsights.title: Time matters more than distance
discoveryInsights.detail: If the guest is exposed briefly during boarding, swimming, tender transfer, or deck service, valuable footage may already exist.

discoveryInsights.title: Itinerary leaks create the window
discoveryInsights.detail: Crew chatter, AIS patterns, marina staff, provisioning, tender schedules, pilots, restaurants, and predictable anchorages can create the attack window.

discoveryInsights.title: The yacht signature attracts the drone
discoveryInsights.detail: A famous yacht, support vessel, helicopter, tender, or escort pattern can reveal the target even if the guest is hidden.

ideas.title: Build a privacy envelope
ideas.detail: Pre-map camera cones around the vessel and expose the guest only in zones blocked by superstructure, screens, awnings, shore geometry, or backlighting.

ideas.title: Treat transfers as the critical event
ideas.detail: Use covered tender docks, enclosed passerelles, privacy canopies, garage-to-garage tender movement, and timing that degrades useful footage.

ideas.title: Use passive early warning
ideas.detail: Remote ID receiver, RF detection, visual observer, marine radar/operator reports, and acoustic/optical watch exist to move the guest before useful capture.

ideas.title: Shift intervention to the operator
ideas.detail: Identify launch or control locations and use marina security, port authority, coast guard, police, or private investigators where lawful.

ideas.title: Design deck use like film blocking in reverse
ideas.detail: Avoid exposed rail lines, celebrity-facing open decks near shore, uncovered transitions, and high-contrast hero angles.

nextTests: Map plausible drone launch points and simulate camera lines to guest-use zones.
Run a red-team exercise with a legal consumer drone or long-lens camera from shore and chase-boat positions.
Time exposure windows for embarkation, tendering, swimming, deck dining, helicopter operations, and crew service.
Test passive detection range and false positives for Remote ID, RF, visual watch, marina, anchorage, and open-water conditions.
Audit the itinerary leak chain: AIS behavior, marina bookings, provisioning vendors, crew phones, social posts, tender patterns, and local gossip nodes.
